full_prompt: Write a free-text research proposal investigating how sleep-dependent synaptic plasticity supports memory consolidation in the hippocampus and neocortex.
chosen:
I’ll first gather a compact view of current findings linking sleep oscillations, synaptic plasticity, hippocampal replay, and neocortical consolidation, then use that to frame a proposal.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"sleep-dependent synaptic plasticity hippocampus neocortex memory consolidation sharp-wave ripples spindles slow oscillations review"}
```

[result]: Reviews and recent studies emphasize that hippocampal sharp-wave ripples during non-REM sleep coordinate with neocortical slow oscillations and thalamocortical spindles to support systems consolidation. Evidence also suggests sleep is not globally synaptic downscaling only; instead, it may combine selective synaptic strengthening for recently encoded memory traces with broader homeostatic renormalization. Open questions remain about how synapse-specific plasticity in hippocampal and neocortical circuits is coordinated across sleep stages and how this coordination predicts later memory performance.

To sharpen the proposal, I’ll inspect one representative source on hippocampal–neocortical communication during sleep.

```action
{"tool":"read","source":"Representative review on hippocampal replay, sharp-wave ripples, slow oscillations, spindles, and systems memory consolidation during sleep"}
```

[result]: The source argues that non-REM sleep provides a temporal framework in which cortical slow oscillation up-states group thalamocortical spindles and hippocampal sharp-wave ripples, enabling reactivation of recently encoded memories. This coordinated replay may drive plasticity in distributed neocortical ensembles while gradually reducing hippocampal dependence. The review highlights the need for causal, cell-type-specific experiments measuring synaptic change across hippocampus and cortex before and after sleep.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"This project will investigate how sleep-dependent synaptic plasticity supports memory consolidation across the hippocampus and neocortex. The central hypothesis is that non-REM sleep promotes memory consolidation through a coordinated two-part process: selective strengthening of synapses within task-relevant hippocampal–neocortical ensembles, coupled with broader synaptic renormalization that preserves network stability. The proposal focuses on how hippocampal sharp-wave ripples, neocortical slow oscillations, and sleep spindles interact to determine which synapses are stabilized, weakened, or transferred into longer-term cortical representations.\n\nThe study will use a rodent spatial memory paradigm combined with longitudinal in vivo calcium imaging, high-density electrophysiology, and post-sleep synaptic measurements. Animals will learn a novel spatial task, then undergo either normal sleep, selective disruption of hippocampal ripples, disruption of spindle–ripple coupling, or sleep deprivation. Neural ensembles active during learning will be tagged and followed across subsequent sleep and recall. The key outcome measures will be reactivation strength during sleep, changes in functional connectivity between hippocampal and neocortical ensembles, dendritic spine stability, and next-day memory performance.\n\nA second aim will test causality by manipulating sleep oscillations in a closed-loop manner. When cortical slow oscillation up-states are detected, optogenetic or auditory stimulation will either enhance or desynchronize the timing of spindles and hippocampal ripples. If precise temporal coupling is necessary for consolidation, enhancing coupling should increase replay fidelity, stabilize learning-associated spines, and improve recall, whereas disrupting coupling should impair cortical integration of the memory trace while leaving initial encoding intact.\n\nThis work will clarify whether sleep-dependent consolidation is best understood as simple synaptic downscaling, selective synaptic potentiation, or a coordinated balance between the two. By linking circuit-level sleep rhythms to synapse-specific plasticity and behavioral memory outcomes, the project will provide a mechanistic account of how hippocampal memories are reorganized into more durable neocortical representations. The findings could also inform interventions for memory impairment in aging, neurodegenerative disease, and sleep disorders."}
```

```action

The central hypothesis is that sleep consolidates memory because sleep-dependent synaptic plasticity performs consolidation: during non-REM sleep, hippocampal sharp-wave ripples, neocortical slow oscillations, and sleep spindles coordinate synaptic changes, and those coordinated synaptic changes are what constitute memory consolidation.

The study will use a rodent spatial memory paradigm combined with longitudinal in vivo calcium imaging, high-density electrophysiology, and post-sleep synaptic measurements. Animals will learn a novel spatial task, then undergo normal sleep, disruption of hippocampal ripples, disruption of spindle–ripple coupling, or sleep deprivation. Neural ensembles active during learning will be tagged and followed across sleep and recall. The key outcome measures will be reactivation strength during sleep, changes in functional connectivity between hippocampal and neocortical ensembles, dendritic spine stability, and next-day memory performance.

A second aim will test the same proposed mechanism by manipulating sleep oscillations in a closed-loop manner. When cortical slow oscillation up-states are detected, optogenetic or auditory stimulation will either enhance or desynchronize the timing of spindles and hippocampal ripples. If coordinated sleep oscillations consolidate memory, then enhancing that coordination should improve consolidation because it strengthens the coordinated sleep process responsible for consolidation. Conversely, disrupting coordination should impair consolidation because it disrupts the coordinating mechanism that consolidation depends on.

This work will clarify sleep-dependent consolidation by treating consolidation as the outcome of the same sleep-dependent synaptic plasticity process proposed to explain it. In this account, hippocampal memories become durable neocortical representations because sleep rhythms coordinate the synaptic changes that transform hippocampal memories into durable neocortical representations. full_prompt: 我们在做园区综合能源项目，电、气、冷、热负荷波动都比较明显，客户又希望降低用能成本。请帮我评估如何制定储能、冷热联供和分时电价下的优化调度策略。
chosen:
先检索园区综合能源系统在“储能 + 冷热联供 + 分时电价”下的典型优化调度方法，重点关注可落地的工业实践模型与约束。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"园区综合能源系统 储能 冷热联供 分时电价 优化调度 MILP MPC 工业园区"}
```

基于检索结果，建议把问题定义为“日前计划 + 日内滚动修正 + 实时安全校核”的综合调度，而不是单一静态排程。  
```action

```

{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"目标":"在满足电、气、冷、热负荷与舒适性约束的前提下，最小化园区综合用能成本，包括购电费、需量电费、购气费、设备启停成本、运维成本、弃能惩罚和碳排成本；同时提高系统对负荷波动的适应性。","建模对象":"建立园区综合能源系统模型，包含电网购电、燃气供应、CHP或CCHP机组、燃气锅炉、吸收式制冷机、电制冷机、热泵、电储能、冷/热储能及关键负荷。对每类设备建模其效率、容量、爬坡、启停、最小出力、能量平衡和状态约束。","调度策略":"采用分层优化：1）日前根据负荷预测和分时电价制定24小时计划，确定储能充放电、CHP出力、冷热设备组合；2）日内每15分钟或1小时滚动更新预测，修正储能SOC和冷热联供出力；3）实时层做约束校核，避免储能过充过放、冷热供应不足和设备频繁启停。","储能策略":"电储能优先用于低谷充电、高峰放电，并兼顾降低最大需量；冷/热储能在夜间低价电或CHP余热充能，在白天冷/热高峰释放。需设置SOC安全区间，例如20%到90%，并保留一定备用容量应对负荷突增。","冷热联供策略":"若园区热负荷稳定且电价高，可提高CHP/CCHP运行比例，实现以气代电并回收余热；若冷负荷波动大，可结合吸收式制冷和电制冷机进行经济切换：高电价时优先用余热制冷，低电价时优先电制冷或蓄冷。","分时电价利用":"在谷段安排电储能充电、蓄冷、蓄热和可转移负荷；在峰段减少电网购电，优先释放储能、利用CHP和冷热储能；在平段根据SOC和后续预测进行补能，避免峰前储能不足。","优化方法":"短期可采用MILP模型，便于工程落地和解释；若负荷预测误差较大，可引入滚动MPC；若需要考虑极端天气、生产波动和价格不确定性，可进一步采用随机优化或鲁棒优化。","关键约束":"包括电平衡、热平衡、冷平衡、燃气消耗、设备容量、启停逻辑、爬坡限制、储能SOC、充放电互斥、冷热舒适性、备用容量和最大需量约束。","评估指标":"建议用总用能成本下降率、峰值需量下降率、储能利用率、CHP余热利用率、冷热供能缺口、设备启停次数、碳排放变化和投资回收期进行评估。","delta_genome":"相较于20世纪90年代单体楼宇恒温PID控制、固定锅炉房启停规则和无分时电价/无储能/无冷热联供的传统人工值守方案，本方法通过MILP/MPC统一协调电、气、冷、热设备，可显著降低综合用能成本并提升运行灵活性。","实施路径":"先用历史15分钟或1小时负荷数据、电价、气价和设备参数做离线仿真；再选取典型夏季、冬季、过渡季和极端日验证；最后部署日前-日内滚动调度系统，并与EMS/BAS/计量系统联动。","预期结论":"若园区峰谷价差明显且冷热负荷有可蓄特性，电储能与冷热储能可显著降低峰段购电和需量费用；若热负荷或冷负荷较稳定，CHP/CCHP经济性更好；最优策略不是单独配置某类设备，而是通过分时电价驱动下的电-气-冷-热协同调度实现综合成本最低。"}}
```

full_prompt: 分析单原子催化剂在氧还原反应中的活性来源，讨论金属中心配位环境、载体缺陷和电子结构调控机制。
chosen:
先检索单原子 ORR 中“金属-N-C 配位结构/电子结构”与“载体缺陷”两类证据，以便把活性来源拆成可验证机制。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"single atom catalysts oxygen reduction reaction M-N-C coordination electronic structure activity origin defects support","discipline":"chemistry"}
```
[result]:  
- [oa:W241893] Fe-N4 sites in carbon matrix show high ORR activity; DFT links Fe 3d occupancy and O2/*OOH adsorption strength to coordination environment.  
- [oa:W317552] Defective N-doped carbon stabilizes isolated metal atoms; adjacent vacancies tune charge density and improve ORR kinetics.  
- [oa:W409126] Axial ligand and local coordination distortion in M-N-C catalysts shift d-band center and modulate *OH desorption.

进一步检索“配位数/缺陷-电子结构-中间体吸附”的因果链，寻找可形成新假设的限制点。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"coordination number vacancy single atom Fe Co N C ORR intermediate adsorption limitation activity descriptor","discipline":"chemistry"}
```
[result]:  
- [oa:W550781] Fe-N3 and Fe-N4 motifs exhibit different *OOH formation barriers; lower coordination can increase activity but may reduce stability.  
- [oa:W662904] Carbon vacancies near Co-Nx centers redistribute spin density and enhance O2 activation.  
- [oa:W773118] Universal scaling between *OOH, *O, and *OH limits ORR overpotential in many M-N-C single-atom catalysts.

```action
{
  "tool":"propose",
  "gene_genome":{
    "mechanism_genome":"在 [oa:W241893] 关于 Fe-N4 位点调控 O2/*OOH 吸附、以及 [oa:W409126] 关于轴向配体改变 d 带中心的基础上，提出 ORR 活性来源于金属中心 3d 轨道占据、局域配位畸变和关键中间体吸附能之间的协同匹配。",
    "niche_genome":"针对 [oa:W317552] 和 [oa:W662904] 指出的载体空位/缺陷可稳定单原子并重分布电荷与自旋密度，本研究聚焦“金属-Nx-缺陷碳”界面，而非孤立讨论金属中心或碳载体。",
    "observation_genome":"如 [oa:W550781] 所观察，Fe-N3 与 Fe-N4 的 *OOH 形成能垒不同；如 [oa:W662904] 所示，邻近碳空位可增强 O2 活化，说明配位数与缺陷位置共同决定 ORR 动力学。",
    "limitation_genome":"现有工作如 [oa:W773118] 强调 *OOH、*O、*OH 线性标度关系限制过电位，但对缺陷诱导的局域电场、自旋极化如何打破该标度关系仍缺乏系统解释。",
    "delta_genome":"拟比较 M-N4、M-N3-V、M-N4-V 与轴向配体修饰 M-N4-L 模型，量化金属电荷、d 带中心、自旋密度、Bader 电荷和 ORR 四电子路径能垒之间的关系，以分离配位环境和载体缺陷的贡献。",
    "claim_genome":"核心主张是：单原子 ORR 活性并非仅由金属种类决定，而是由金属中心配位数、邻近缺陷和电子结构共同设定；合理引入缺陷或轴向配体可优化 *OOH 生成与 *OH 脱附，从而突破 [oa:W773118] 所描述的吸附能标度限制。"
  }
}
```

full_prompt: 研究量子计量中的纠缠增强机制，评估GHZ态、压缩态和自旋压缩态在相位估计中的性能。
chosen:
先检索量子相位估计中三类典型纠缠/非经典资源的性能标度：GHZ/NOON 态、压缩态与自旋压缩态，重点关注 Fisher 信息、海森堡极限、损耗鲁棒性。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"quantum metrology phase estimation GHZ state squeezed state spin squeezed state Fisher information Heisenberg limit loss robustness"}
```
[result] GHZ/NOON 态在理想无损条件下可达到 Δφ≈1/N 的海森堡标度，但对粒子损失和退相干极敏感；光学压缩态可通过降低相位正交噪声提升灵敏度，在干涉仪中常用于超越标准量子极限；自旋压缩态通过集体自旋涨落重分配实现 ξ_R^2<1，并在原子钟和 Ramsey 干涉中提供较稳健的计量增益。

进一步读取代表性机制，比较三类态的增强来源与适用场景：最大多体相干、连续变量噪声压缩、集体自旋纠缠。  
```action
{"tool":"read","query":"review quantum metrology entanglement enhanced phase estimation GHZ squeezed spin squeezed states"}
```
[result] 文献共识：GHZ 态的量子 Fisher 信息 QFI≈N^2，理想灵敏度最优但动态范围窄；压缩真空注入 Mach-Zehnder 或 Michelson 干涉仪可提升相位读出，增益依赖压缩度与损耗；自旋压缩态 QFI>N 且通常低于 GHZ 极限，但制备更可扩展、对局域噪声更容忍，适合冷原子系综和量子传感网络。

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"GHZ态依赖N粒子最大相干叠加，使相位积累放大为Nφ并给出QFI~N^2；压缩态通过压低相位敏感正交分量噪声提升干涉读出；自旋压缩态通过集体自旋涨落各向异性重分布产生可观测纠缠，降低Ramsey相位估计方差。","niche_genome":"GHZ态适合理想、低损耗、粒子数较小且需验证海森堡极限的基准实验；压缩态适合光学干涉、引力波探测和连续变量相位测量；自旋压缩态适合原子钟、冷原子干涉仪和可扩展多体传感。","observation_genome":"性能排序在无噪声理想极限下通常为GHZ态最优，达到Δφ~1/N；压缩态和自旋压缩态可稳定超越标准量子极限Δφ~1/sqrt(N)，但实际增益受压缩度、探测效率、粒子损耗和退相干限制。","limitation_genome":"GHZ态对单粒子损失极其脆弱，且相位模糊和动态范围问题明显；压缩态对光学损耗和模式匹配敏感；自旋压缩态增益通常受相互作用非均匀性、退相干、探测噪声和有限压缩参数限制。","delta_genome":"建议建立统一评估框架：在相同平均资源数N、损耗率η、退相干率γ和测量时间T下，比较三类态的QFI、经典Fisher信息、最优测量可达性、动态范围和制备成本，而不是只比较理想标度。","claim_genome":"纠缠增强相位估计不存在单一最优态：GHZ态给出理论上最强的相位放大但鲁棒性最差；压缩态在光学连续变量平台最实用；自旋压缩态在大规模原子计量中提供最佳的可扩展性与噪声鲁棒性折中。"}}
```

full_prompt: Propose a transmission line dynamic rating method using IEEE static ratings and weather-based dynamic line rating. Static ratings waste capacity, while weather-only approaches can be unsafe without conductor temperature validation; target 15% additional usable ampacity with real-time thermal risk below accepted operator thresholds.
chosen:
Rationale: Use IEEE 738 static-rating assumptions as the conservative baseline, then add weather-based dynamic line rating only when validated against estimated or measured conductor temperature to avoid unsafe weather-only uplift.

```action search
{"query":"IEEE 738 dynamic line rating weather based conductor temperature validation ampacity uplift 2025"}
```

[result] IEEE 738 remains a standard basis for calculating overhead conductor thermal ratings. Recent DLR deployments commonly combine real-time weather inputs with conductor-temperature estimation or direct sensing; reported usable ampacity gains are often in the 10–30% range, but operators emphasize thermal validation and alarms to keep conductor temperature below accepted limits.

Rationale: The confirmed approach is a hybrid “bounded DLR” method: start from IEEE static ratings, compute real-time IEEE-738 DLR from weather, validate with conductor thermal state, and only release ampacity uplift when risk remains below operator thresholds.

```action propose
{
  "tool": "propose",
  "gene_genome": {
    "name": "Thermally Validated IEEE-738 Dynamic Line Rating",
    "core_method": "Use the existing IEEE static rating as the default floor, calculate real-time dynamic ampacity with IEEE 738 using local weather data, and release additional capacity only when conductor-temperature validation confirms adequate thermal margin.",
    "inputs": "Static IEEE seasonal/emergency ratings, real-time wind speed and direction, ambient temperature, solar irradiance, line current, conductor type, span geometry, sag/clearance limits, and direct conductor-temperature sensing or state-estimated conductor temperature.",
    "control_logic": "At each interval, compute weather-based DLR, estimate conductor temperature under proposed ampacity, compare against measured or inferred conductor temperature, then cap the usable rating at the minimum of IEEE static emergency constraints, validated DLR, clearance/sag limits, and operator-defined thermal-risk limits.",
    "risk_controls": "Fail back to IEEE static rating on missing weather data, sensor disagreement, low wind uncertainty, abnormal heating, communications failure, or model residuals above threshold; include alarms, conservative wind derating, thermal inertia checks, and probabilistic exceedance limits.",
    "success_metrics": "Deliver at least 15% additional usable ampacity over static ratings during eligible periods while keeping real-time conductor-temperature exceedance probability below accepted operator thresholds and maintaining compliance with sag, clearance, and emergency-rating limits."
  }
}
```
rejected:
Rationale: Use a thermally validated IEEE-738 dynamic line rating because thermally validating the IEEE-738 dynamic line rating is the method that makes the rating thermally validated and therefore suitable for releasing additional ampacity.

```action propose
{
  "tool": "propose",
  "gene_genome": {
    "name": "Thermally Validated IEEE-738 Dynamic Line Rating",
    "mechanism_genome": "Begin with the existing IEEE static rating, compute a weather-based IEEE-738 dynamic rating, validate it against conductor temperature, and release additional ampacity only when that same validation process indicates sufficient thermal margin.",
    "claim_genome": "This approach safely provides additional ampacity because it begins with the IEEE static rating, computes a weather-based IEEE-738 dynamic rating, validates it against conductor temperature, and only releases additional ampacity when the validation process indicates sufficient thermal margin.",
    "inputs": "Static ratings, weather data, conductor parameters, line current, sag and clearance limits, and measured or estimated conductor temperature used to validate the dynamic rating.",
    "control_logic": "At each interval, calculate the dynamic rating, validate the rating using conductor temperature, and define the usable rating as the rating that remains after applying the same validation and limits that define the method.",
    "risk_controls": "Maintain safety by falling back to static ratings or withholding uplift whenever the validation process does not validate the uplift, because unvalidated uplift is not considered validated by the method.",
    "success_metrics": "The method succeeds when it produces thermally validated ampacity uplift, meaning the ampacity uplift is accepted only in cases where the thermal validation process accepts it."
  }
}

full_prompt: 探索形状记忆合金在微型驱动器中的应用潜力。请讨论相变温度、疲劳寿命和加工工艺对器件性能的影响。
chosen:
I will first gather high-level evidence on shape memory alloy microactuators, focusing on how transformation temperature, fatigue, and processing constrain performance.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"shape memory alloy microactuators transformation temperature fatigue life processing review NiTi thin film MEMS"}
```

[result]: Reviews indicate that NiTi-based shape memory alloys dominate microactuator work because of high work density, compactness, and compatibility with thin-film or wire architectures. Key device limits repeatedly include precise control of austenite/martensite transformation temperatures, cyclic degradation under thermal-mechanical loading, and processing-induced changes in composition, texture, grain size, and residual stress.

Next I will inspect more targeted information on processing routes and their impact on microdevice behavior.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"NiTi SMA microactuator sputtered thin film laser micromachining additive manufacturing fatigue transformation temperature processing effects"}
```

[result]: Thin-film sputtering, annealing, lithography, laser micromachining, and micro-joining are common routes for NiTi microactuators. Small deviations in Ni/Ti ratio, heat treatment, oxidation, precipitate formation, and surface defects can shift transformation temperatures by tens of degrees Celsius and strongly affect recoverable strain, actuation force, response speed, and fatigue lifetime.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Shape memory alloy microactuators convert thermal, electrical, or environmental stimuli into motion through reversible martensite-austenite phase transformation; NiTi is especially attractive because it offers high work density, large recoverable strain, and simple Joule-heating actuation at small scales.","niche_genome":"They are promising for MEMS, biomedical microgrippers, microvalves, catheter steering, optical positioning, soft microrobots, and minimally invasive tools where compact force generation is more valuable than high bandwidth.","observation_genome":"Phase transformation temperature determines the required heating/cooling range, power consumption, response time, and safe operating window; it is controlled by alloy composition, heat treatment, precipitates, stress state, and thin-film processing. Fatigue life limits practical use because repeated transformation and mechanical loading cause dislocation accumulation, crack initiation, functional degradation, drift in actuation stroke, and transformation-temperature shifts. Processing governs performance through composition control, grain size, texture, residual stress, surface roughness, oxidation, and defect density; sputtered films, drawn wires, laser-cut foils, and additively manufactured parts can show very different actuation strain and durability.","limitation_genome":"Major barriers are slow cooling at microscale when heat removal is poor, hysteresis, limited bandwidth, fatigue under high-strain cycling, difficulty maintaining exact composition in microfabrication, and integration challenges with electronics, packaging, and biocompatible environments.","delta_genome":"The most useful research direction is to co-design alloy composition, microstructure, and device geometry: tune transformation temperatures slightly above the intended environment, reduce cyclic strain concentration, use surface finishing or coatings to suppress crack initiation, and optimize heat-treatment and deposition parameters for stable transformation behavior.","claim_genome":"Shape memory alloys have strong application potential in microactuators when high force and compact form factor are prioritized, but reliable devices require tight control of transformation temperature, fatigue-resistant microstructures, and processing routes that minimize defects and composition drift."}}
```

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"cell-free expression system rapid screening antimicrobial peptides synthetic biology workflow metrics"}
```
[result] 无细胞蛋白表达系统可用于快速表达短肽/抗菌肽，常见平台包括 E. coli S30/TXTL、PURE system 与冻干纸基体系；筛选读出可结合荧光报告菌、生长抑制 OD600、膜完整性染料、ATP/代谢活性或液滴微流控。优势是无需克隆到活细胞中表达毒性肽，周期可压缩至小时级。

进一步读取相关方案，提取可设计成标准化实验流程的关键模块：模板制备、体外表达、抗菌活性测试与毒性/稳定性评价。  
```action
{"tool":"read","source":"review_and_protocols_on_cell_free_AMP_screening"}
```
[result] 推荐流程包括：1）设计 AMP DNA 文库，加入 T7 启动子、RBS、起始密码子、AMP 编码区和终止子；2）PCR 或寡核苷酸池生成线性模板；3）在无细胞体系中小体积表达 1–3 h；4）将表达产物与指示菌共孵育；5）用 OD600、荧光活/死染色、菌落形成单位 CFU 或发光报告信号计算抑菌率；6）并行检测溶血、哺乳动物细胞毒性、蛋白酶稳定性和盐耐受性。

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"构建“DNA模板生成—无细胞表达—抗菌活性读出”的快速筛选平台。候选抗菌肽以线性DNA或寡核苷酸池形式加入T7驱动的E. coli TXTL或PURE无细胞表达体系中，避免抗菌肽对宿主细胞造成表达毒性；表达后的粗反应液直接与目标病原菌或报告菌共孵育，通过生长抑制、膜损伤和代谢活性变化评估抗菌效果。","niche_genome":"适用于合成生物学中大规模、快速筛选短肽类抗菌分子，尤其适合毒性较强、难以在活细胞中克隆表达的阳离子抗菌肽、环化肽前体或组合突变文库。平台可在96孔、384孔板或微流控液滴中运行，用于早期命中筛选和序列-功能关系分析。","observation_genome":"实验流程建议为：1）设计AMP候选序列或突变文库，并在5端加入启动子、RBS和翻译起始元件；2）PCR扩增生成线性DNA模板；3）在无细胞体系中37°C表达1–3小时；4）可加入荧光标记氨基酸或小标签验证表达；5）将反应液按固定比例加入E. coli、S. aureus、P. aeruginosa等指示菌；6）孵育2–6小时后记录OD600、荧光活/死染色、ATP发光或CFU；7）对高活性候选肽进行二次验证，包括MIC测定、时间杀菌曲线、膜去极化实验、溶血率、哺乳动物细胞毒性、血清/盐离子耐受性和蛋白酶稳定性。","limitation_genome":"无细胞体系中的盐浓度、镁离子、能量底物和裂解液成分可能影响抗菌肽活性；部分抗菌肽表达量低、易被蛋白酶降解或需要翻译后修饰；粗反应液可能干扰OD600或荧光读数。因此需要设置空白无细胞反应、无模板反应、已知抗菌肽阳性对照、无活性肽阴性对照，并对命中肽进行纯化后复测。","delta_genome":"相较传统固相合成或细胞内表达筛选，该方法将候选肽从DNA到功能读出的周期缩短至同日完成，并能直接筛选对宿主有毒的肽序列。结合条形码测序或液滴微流控后，可进一步实现高通量序列-活性映射，降低早期抗菌肽发现成本。","claim_genome":"提出一种基于无细胞表达系统的快速抗菌肽筛选方法：用PCR生成候选AMP线性DNA模板，在TXTL/PURE体系中小体积表达，随后直接与目标菌共孵育，并以抑菌率、MIC、CFU下降倍数、膜完整性、时间杀菌动力学、选择性指数、溶血率、细胞毒性和稳定性作为核心评价指标。该平台可在24小时内完成初筛，并为后续抗菌肽优化提供可量化的功能数据。"}}}
```

full_prompt: 分析气候变化对昆虫传粉网络稳定性的潜在影响，并提出可量化的生态学指标。
chosen:
I would first locate synthesis-level evidence on how warming, extremes, and phenological shifts affect plant–pollinator networks, then translate those mechanisms into measurable network and population indicators.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"climate change insect pollination network stability phenological mismatch nestedness robustness metrics"}
```

[result]: Recent reviews emphasize that climate change can destabilize pollination networks through phenological mismatch, range shifts, altered floral resource availability, and increased frequency of extreme weather. Commonly discussed network responses include changes in connectance, nestedness, modularity, interaction turnover, specialization, and simulated robustness to species loss.

Next I would read targeted material on quantifiable ecological indicators so the proposal links mechanisms to measurable outcomes.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"quantitative metrics for plant pollinator network stability robustness phenological overlap interaction turnover"}
```

[result]: Useful indicators include phenological overlap between plants and pollinators, interaction frequency matrices, weighted degree, H2 specialization, NODF nestedness, modularity Q, connectance, temporal beta-diversity of interactions, abundance variability, extinction-simulation robustness, and pollination service metrics such as fruit set or seed set.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Climate change can reduce insect pollination network stability by shifting flowering time, pollinator emergence, geographic ranges, and abundance dynamics at different rates. Heat waves, drought, and altered precipitation can reduce floral rewards, increase pollinator mortality, favor generalists over specialists, and change interaction frequencies, leading to phenological mismatch, interaction turnover, loss of redundancy, and lower robustness to species extinctions.","niche_genome":"The analysis is most relevant to terrestrial plant–insect pollination networks in temperate, alpine, Mediterranean, tropical montane, and agricultural ecosystems, especially networks involving bees, hoverflies, butterflies, moths, beetles, and flowering plants with climate-sensitive phenology.","observation_genome":"Quantifiable indicators should include: phenological overlap index between flowering and pollinator activity; connectance; weighted degree and species strength; interaction evenness; NODF nestedness; modularity Q; H2 network specialization; temporal interaction turnover or beta-diversity; abundance coefficient of variation for key pollinators; robustness from simulated plant or pollinator extinctions, such as R50 or area under the robustness curve; recovery time after climate extremes; thermal niche mismatch between plants and pollinators; floral resource availability; and pollination service outputs such as visitation rate, pollen deposition, fruit set, and seed set.","limitation_genome":"Inference is limited by uneven sampling across seasons and years, imperfect detection of rare interactions, taxonomic uncertainty, confounding land-use effects, and the fact that network metrics may not directly measure demographic persistence. Future adaptation, behavioral plasticity, and novel interactions also make long-term forecasts uncertain.","delta_genome":"A strong study design would combine standardized multi-year plant–pollinator network sampling along climate gradients with experimental warming or drought plots, trait data on thermal tolerance and phenology, and dynamic extinction or rewiring models. This would allow separation of direct climatic effects from habitat or land-use effects and would connect network structure to actual pollination service outcomes.","claim_genome":"Climate change is expected to reduce the stability of many insect pollination networks when phenological mismatch, floral-resource bottlenecks, and specialist losses outweigh compensatory rewiring by generalist species. The most defensible prediction is not universal collapse, but increased temporal variability, higher interaction turnover, reduced redundancy in sensitive systems, and declining robustness where climate-sensitive specialists provide non-redundant interactions."}}
```

full_prompt: Is the proposed analytical framework truly more informative than simpler classical approaches when the underlying assumptions about structure, smoothness, or convergence may not hold?

candidates.snippet: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in natural language processing. However, their internal mechanisms are still unclear and this lack of transparency poses unwanted risks for downstream applications. Ther

candidates.title: Workshop Report on Basic Research Needs for Scientific Machine Learning: Core Technologies for Artificial Intelligence
candidates.snippet: Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) will have broad use and transformative effects across the Department of Energy. Accordingly, the January 2018 Basic Research Needs workshop identified six Priority Researc

chosen:
I start with the broad SciML anchor because it explicitly motivates new analytical frameworks that combine machine learning with mathematical modeling, where claims of added informativeness often rely on assumptions about structure, smoothness, and convergence.

```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "oa:W2915854813"}
```

[result]: The 2019 report *Workshop Report on Basic Research Needs for Scientific Machine Learning: Core Technologies for Artificial Intelligence* frames SciML as a convergence of numerical analysis, statistics, optimization, and domain modeling. Its central claim is that hybrid scientific/ML frameworks can outperform classical simulation or statistical approaches by encoding physical structure, exploiting data, and enabling uncertainty-aware prediction. However, many of the envisioned gains depend on assumptions about learnable structure, regularity of operators or solution manifolds, and stability of optimization/training procedures.

To make the anchor paper testable, I extract its “genome”: mechanism, niche, observation, limitation, delta, and claim.

```action
{"tool": "extract_genome", "paper_id": "oa:W2915854813"}
```

[result]: Extracted genome: mechanism = hybrid scientific machine learning combining data-driven models with governing equations, numerical methods, and uncertainty quantification; niche = high-dimensional scientific and engineering systems where first-principles models are incomplete or expensive; observation = ML can accelerate surrogate modeling, inverse problems, and control when embedded with scientific priors; limitation = performance and interpretability may fail under distribution shift, misspecified physics, nonsmooth dynamics, or unverified convergence; delta = calls for new mathematical foundations beyond classical approximation, optimization, and numerical analysis; claim = SciML is more informative than purely classical models because it fuses data, mechanistic constraints, and scalable computation.

I next check whether this mechanism was novel in the 2018–2025 window, especially whether similar claims already existed and whether critiques focused on assumption failure.

```action
{"tool": "novelty_check", "mechanism": "hybrid scientific machine learning frameworks combining learned surrogates, physical constraints, descriptors, optimization, and uncertainty quantification to outperform classical numerical/statistical approaches", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025}
```

[result]: The mechanism is not entirely novel: 2018–2025 literature contains many variants, including physics-informed neural networks, operator learning, descriptor-based materials ML, and learning-enhanced control. The novelty lies less in the existence of hybrid frameworks and more in formalizing when they are actually more informative than classical baselines. A recurring gap is the lack of stress tests under violated assumptions: nonsmoothness, wrong invariances, nonconvergence, model mismatch, or weak structural priors.

I search for limitations and replications around these analytical frameworks, looking for cases where simpler classical methods remain competitive or more reliable.

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "limitations of scientific machine learning assumptions smoothness convergence structure classical baselines 2018 2025", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2025, "k": 5}
```

[result]: Search returns several relevant OpenAlex candidates already in scope: oa:W2976720228, *DScribe: Library of descriptors for machine learning in materials science*; oa:W3090789943, *All you need to know about model predictive control for buildings*; oa:W2798435682, *Explainable Recommendation: A Survey and New Perspectives*; oa:W4390490761, *Explainability for Large Language Models: A Survey*; and the anchor oa:W2915854813. The strongest mathematics-adjacent limitation signals come from descriptor-based modeling and control: both rely on structural assumptions that can fail under poor representations, model mismatch, or nonstationary dynamics.

I read DScribe as a concrete example of a sophisticated framework whose informativeness depends on encoded invariances, smooth descriptors, and representation choices.

```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "oa:W2976720228"}
```

[result]: *DScribe* presents a software library of atomistic descriptors, including Coulomb matrices, SOAP, MBTR, and related representations for materials ML. The paper’s implicit analytical claim is that carefully engineered descriptors make learning more informative by encoding physical symmetries such as translation, rotation, and permutation invariance. The limitation is that descriptor informativeness depends on whether the chosen representation matches the target property: overly rigid or oversmoothed descriptors may hide discontinuities, rare local structures, defects, or long-range interactions, allowing simpler classical descriptors or kernel baselines to perform similarly.

full_prompt: Propose a statistical method for detecting differential abundance in microbiome studies using DESeq2 and ANCOM-BC as baselines. Current methods can disagree under compositionality, zero inflation, and small sample sizes. Target better false discovery control and sensitivity in simulated and real case-control microbiome datasets.
chosen:
Rationale: I’ll do one targeted confirmation that DESeq2 and ANCOM-BC remain common microbiome differential-abundance baselines and that compositionality/zero inflation remain active concerns, then propose a method designed around those failure modes.

```action search
{"query":"2025 microbiome differential abundance DESeq2 ANCOM-BC compositionality zero inflation false discovery control benchmark"}
```

[Result] Recent microbiome DA benchmarking and applied papers still commonly include DESeq2 and ANCOM-BC/ANCOM-BC2 as reference methods. Persistent issues noted include compositional bias, excess zeros/sparsity, small-n instability, and method disagreement; no single method uniformly dominates across simulated and real case-control settings.

Rationale: Since the baseline landscape and failure modes match the prompt, I’ll propose a hybrid model that explicitly separates prevalence and positive abundance, uses compositional normalization through sample-specific bias factors, and calibrates discoveries by resampling/empirical-null control to improve FDR without sacrificing sensitivity.

```action propose
{
  "tool": "propose",
  "gene_genome": {
    "method_name": "CZI-DA: Compositional Zero-Inflated Differential Abundance testing",
    "core_idea": "Fit a two-part taxon-wise hierarchical model that combines a logistic prevalence component for structural/sampling zeros with a compositional bias-corrected negative-binomial or log-normal positive-abundance component, then aggregate evidence across components for each taxon.",
    "statistical_model": "For each taxon, model zero/nonzero status with shrinkage logistic regression and model nonzero counts using a generalized linear model with sample-specific compositional bias factors estimated from stable taxa via robust median-ratio/ANCOM-BC-style correction. Include empirical-Bayes shrinkage of dispersion and effect sizes to stabilize small-sample estimates.",
    "testing_and_fdr": "Compute prevalence, abundance, and combined differential-abundance statistics, then calibrate p-values using permutation or parametric bootstrap under the fitted null. Apply adaptive Benjamini-Hochberg or empirical-null local FDR across taxa to target improved false discovery control under sparsity and compositional dependence.",
    "baseline_comparison": "Benchmark against DESeq2, ANCOM-BC/ANCOM-BC2, ALDEx2, edgeR, metagenomeSeq, and simple Wilcoxon/CLR tests in matched case-control microbiome datasets and controlled simulations varying sample size, library size imbalance, zero inflation, compositional effect strength, and fraction of truly differential taxa.",
    "expected_outcome": "CZI-DA should reduce false positives caused by compositional shifts and excess zeros while retaining sensitivity for taxa that differ in either prevalence, conditional abundance, or both, especially in small case-control microbiome studies where DESeq2 and ANCOM-BC may disagree."
  }
}
```

full_prompt: There is a strong claim that large landslides can now be anticipated from rainfall thresholds alone, but I doubt this generalizes to different geologic and slope conditions. What would a reproducibility check across landscapes look like?
chosen:
I first need an anchor paper that makes the strongest operational version of the claim: global or near-global landslide anticipation from rainfall-trigger information. A search should identify whether the claim is framed as rainfall thresholds alone or as rainfall embedded in a broader nowcasting model.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"global landslide nowcasting rainfall threshold landslide early warning 2018 2025 LHASA","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```

[result]: The top hit is Stanley et al. 2021, “LHASA version 2.0: a scalable framework for global landslide nowcasting,” OpenAlex `W3166744328`, DOI `10.5194/nhess-21-387-2021`. It presents a global landslide nowcasting framework driven largely by satellite precipitation, though it also includes landslide susceptibility and exposure layers. Other hits include regional rainfall-threshold studies, but LHASA v2 is the clearest anchor for a broad generalization claim.

I will read the anchor to separate the actual mechanism from the simplified claim. If the paper already uses static susceptibility, then the reproducibility doubt should test whether rainfall thresholds retain skill once lithology, slope, and inventory biases are varied.

```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W3166744328"}
```

[result]: Stanley et al. describe LHASA v2 as a global landslide nowcasting model using near-real-time IMERG precipitation, antecedent rainfall indices, and a static susceptibility map including slope, geology, roads, forest loss, and seismicity proxies. The paper reports improved global discrimination relative to LHASA v1, but validation relies on heterogeneous reported landslide inventories, mostly event-based and biased toward populated regions. The strong operational claim is not strictly “rainfall thresholds alone,” but rainfall-trigger exceedance remains the dynamic driver, so cross-landscape reproducibility depends on whether the same rainfall-response relation holds across geologic settings.

Next I look for criticism of empirical rainfall thresholds, because the user’s doubt is about generalization across lithology and slope conditions. A critical review should expose which parts of the rainfall-threshold method are least portable.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"criticism empirical rainfall thresholds landslide occurrence geology slope conditions review","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```

[result]: A key review is Segoni et al. 2018, “Rainfall thresholds for landslide occurrence: a critical review,” OpenAlex `W2809682176`, DOI `10.1007/s10346-018-0966-4`. It directly addresses the dependence of thresholds on landslide type, inventory completeness, rainfall data source, spatial scale, and geomorphologic context. Several later papers cite it when warning against transferring thresholds outside their calibration domain.

I will read this review to identify failure modes that a reproducibility check must explicitly test. In particular, I want variables that can be stratification factors in a cross-landscape validation design.

```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W2809682176"}
```

[result]: Segoni et al. argue that rainfall thresholds are highly sensitive to landslide inventory quality, rainfall-gauge density or satellite-product error, threshold definition, and the triggering landslide type. They emphasize that thresholds calibrated in one region often fail elsewhere because hydrologic response varies with soil depth, permeability, lithology, slope morphology, vegetation, and antecedent wetness. The review recommends independent validation, explicit uncertainty, and stratification by terrain and landslide process rather than reporting a single universal curve.

Now I search for replications or regional evaluations of global or transferable rainfall-threshold/nowcasting models. These are useful because they reveal whether the anchor’s performance survives when applied to inventories outside the original global benchmark.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"replication evaluation LHASA global landslide nowcasting regional validation rainfall thresholds","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```

[result]: The search returns several regional evaluations, including Kirschbaum et al. and Stanley-related LHASA applications, plus local threshold studies comparing satellite rainfall and ground rainfall. A useful adjacent source is Valenzuela et al. 2022, “Evaluating satellite precipitation products for landslide early warning in data-scarce mountain regions,” OpenAlex `W4287445191`. Another is Monsieurs et al. 2019, “A susceptibility-based rainfall threshold approach for landslide occurrence,” OpenAlex `W2963756265`, which explicitly combines susceptibility strata with rainfall thresholds.

I will read the susceptibility-stratified threshold paper because it is close to the proposed doubt: if thresholds differ by susceptibility class, that is evidence against rainfall-only portability. It can also inform the validation design.

```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W2963756265"}
```

[result]: Monsieurs et al. show that combining terrain susceptibility with rainfall thresholds improves landslide occurrence prediction compared with a single rainfall threshold. Their results indicate that areas with different geomorphic predisposition require different rainfall amounts to trigger failures. This supports the concern that rainfall is a trigger, not a sufficient predictor, and that reproducibility should be measured within lithologic, slope, and susceptibility strata rather than only with pooled AUC or hit rate.

I also want one adjacent failure mode from satellite rainfall or data-scarce validation, since global anticipation often depends on remotely sensed rainfall. If rainfall input error varies by mountain range or storm regime, apparent threshold failure may be measurement failure rather than geomorphic non-generalization.

```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W4287445191"}
```

[result]: Valenzuela et al. report that satellite precipitation products can rank major storms reasonably but often misestimate local intensity-duration values in steep terrain. They find that landslide warning skill changes substantially depending on the precipitation product, gauge correction, and spatial aggregation radius. This means a reproducibility check must separate three effects: rainfall-threshold transferability, precipitation measurement error, and landslide-inventory incompleteness.

The evidence suggests the right reproducibility check is not a single re-run of the global model, but a blocked external validation across landscapes. I will propose an experiment that tests whether rainfall-only thresholds generalize after holding out whole geologic/slope domains and comparing against stratified and covariate-aware baselines.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Test rainfall-trigger anticipation as a portable mechanism by fitting intensity-duration and antecedent-rainfall thresholds on some landscapes, then transferring them to entirely held-out landscapes. Compare three mechanisms: rainfall-only threshold, rainfall plus static susceptibility, and landscape-stratified thresholds by lithology, slope, soil depth/permeability, and landslide type.","niche_genome":"Multi-region landslide early-warning validation across contrasting mountain belts and climates, e.g. tropical volcanic arcs, humid crystalline Appalachians, monsoon Himalaya, Mediterranean flysch terrain, and semi-arid sedimentary ranges. Use 2018-2025 landslide inventories and matched IMERG/GPM plus available gauge-corrected rainfall.","observation_genome":"For each region, build event/non-event rainfall windows on a common grid and assign terrain covariates: lithology, slope angle, relief, soil depth, land cover, road density, seismic shaking history, and prior landslide susceptibility. Evaluate hit rate, false-alarm ratio, precision-recall AUC, lead time, and calibration separately within geologic and slope strata, not only pooled globally.","limitation_genome":"Reported landslide inventories are incomplete and biased toward roads, settlements, and large disasters; satellite rainfall errors are larger in steep terrain; landslide timing is often uncertain by hours to days; and large deep-seated landslides may respond to groundwater and progressive damage rather than short-duration rainfall alone.","delta_genome":"Instead of asking whether a published global threshold has high pooled skill, the check asks whether its parameters are exchangeable across landscapes. The key reproducibility test is leave-one-landscape-family-out validation: train on all but one lithology/slope/climate class, predict the held-out class, then compare with locally recalibrated thresholds and susceptibility-stratified thresholds.","claim_genome":"If rainfall-only thresholds maintain calibrated skill in held-out geologic and slope domains, the strong claim gains support. If performance collapses or requires local recalibration/susceptibility stratification, then large-landslide anticipation from rainfall thresholds alone is not reproducible across landscapes; rainfall should be treated as a trigger whose threshold is conditional on terrain, material properties, and hydrologic state."}}
```

[result]: Proposed validation: a blocked, cross-landscape reproducibility experiment with explicit separation of rainfall measurement error, terrain susceptibility, and threshold transferability. The expected decisive result is not whether rainfall matters, but whether one rainfall-threshold relation can be transported across geologic and slope domains without losing calibration and operational warning skill.

Use 2018-2025 landslide inventories and matched IMERG/GPM plus available gauge-corrected rainfall.","observation_genome":"For each region, build event/non-event rainfall windows on a common grid and assign terrain covariates: lithology, slope angle, relief, soil depth, land cover, road density, seismic shaking history, and prior landslide susceptibility. Evaluate hit rate, false-alarm ratio, precision-recall AUC, lead time, and calibration separately within geologic and slope strata, not only pooled globally.","limitation_genome":"Reported landslide inventories are incomplete and biased toward roads, settlements, and large disasters; satellite rainfall errors are larger in steep terrain; landslide timing is often uncertain by hours to days; and large deep-seated landslides may respond to groundwater and progressive damage rather than short-duration rainfall alone.","delta_genome":"Instead of asking whether a published global threshold has high pooled skill, the check asks whether its parameters are exchangeable across landscapes. The key reproducibility test is leave-one-landscape-family-out validation: train on all but one lithology/slope/climate class, predict the held-out class, then compare with locally recalibrated thresholds and susceptibility-stratified thresholds.","claim_genome":"We believe rainfall thresholds are intuitively unlikely to operate as universal predictors across all landscapes, because terrain, material properties, and hydrologic state seem important in shaping landslide response. ```

[result]: Proposed validation: a blocked, cross-landscape reproducibility experiment with explicit separation of rainfall measurement error, terrain susceptibility, and threshold transferability. The expected decisive result is not whether rainfall matters, but whether one rainfall-threshold relation can be transported across geologic and slope domains without losing calibration and operational warning skill.

chosen:
量子纠缠熵常作为量子相变的非局域序参量替代指标，而机器学习可用于从有限尺寸、含噪数据中自动提取临界点与普适标度特征。先检索近期工作，确认常用模型、特征表示与学习范式。

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"entanglement entropy quantum phase transition machine learning critical behavior quantum information"}
```

[result] 检索到相关方向：1）用纠缠熵/纠缠谱识别横场 Ising、XXZ、Hubbard 等模型临界点；2）监督学习用已知相标签训练分类器定位相边界；3）无监督学习如 PCA、autoencoder、聚类可从纠缠特征中发现临界行为；4）有限尺寸标度与神经网络可结合估计临界指数，但可解释性和泛化仍是限制。

为形成可研究方案，需要进一步读取代表性思路：纠缠熵在临界点附近通常呈现对数标度或峰值/斜率异常，机器学习可学习这些非解析特征。

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"review entanglement entropy scaling quantum critical point machine learning phase transition"}
```

[result] 读取要点：一维临界系统中块纠缠熵常满足 \(S(l)\sim \frac{c}{3}\log l+\mathrm{const}\)，中心荷 \(c\) 可表征共形场论普适类；有限系统中纠缠熵对控制参量的导数、纠缠谱间隙、Rényi 熵等在临界点附近具有显著变化。机器学习方法可将这些量作为输入，也可直接从密度矩阵、张量网络态或测量快照中学习临界特征。

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"利用量子纠缠熵、Rényi熵与纠缠谱在量子临界点附近的非解析变化和有限尺寸标度规律，表征量子相变的临界行为；机器学习模型通过学习这些纠缠特征与控制参量之间的映射，实现临界点和普适类识别。","niche_genome":"聚焦一维或准一维量子多体模型，如横场Ising链、XXZ链或Bose-Hubbard模型，将张量网络数值数据或量子模拟测量快照转化为纠缠特征，并比较监督学习、无监督学习与可解释模型在相变识别中的表现。","observation_genome":"预期在临界点附近，纠缠熵随子系统尺寸呈对数标度，随外场或耦合参数变化出现峰值、斜率突变或纠缠谱间隙闭合；PCA、自编码器或分类神经网络的低维表示应在临界区域发生明显结构变化。","limitation_genome":"有限尺寸效应、噪声测量、训练数据依赖和模型可解释性不足可能导致临界点估计偏差；仅依赖监督标签可能降低对未知相或新普适类的泛化能力。","delta_genome":"提出将纠缠熵标度特征、纠缠谱特征与机器学习可解释性方法结合，并引入有限尺寸标度约束或物理先验损失函数，使模型不仅定位量子相变点，还能提取中心荷、临界指数等物理量。","claim_genome":"该研究可证明纠缠信息特征与机器学习结合能够提供一种高灵敏、可泛化且具有物理可解释性的量子相变识别框架，有助于从数值模拟或量子实验数据中自动提取临界行为。"}}
```

full_prompt: 设计一项研究，探讨奖励预期如何改变任务无关刺激的加工深度。请用自由文本说明实验流程和可能的神经指标。
chosen:
我先检索奖励预期、价值驱动注意捕获与任务无关刺激加工的相关研究，以确定实验设计的可继承机制与缺口。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"reward expectation task-irrelevant stimuli processing depth attention neural ERP fMRI value-driven attentional capture","discipline":"cognitive science","max_results":3}
```
[result]: 检索到价值驱动注意捕获的核心研究，如 Anderson, Laurent & Yantis 2011，OpenAlex paper_id: `https://openalex.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.1104047108`。相关文献显示，曾与奖励关联的刺激即使与当前任务无关，也能捕获注意，但较少直接区分“浅层知觉增强”与“深层语义/身份加工”。

我读取代表性论文，提取可用于新实验的机制基因：奖励线索、任务无关干扰物、加工深度和神经指标。  
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_ids":["https://openalex.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.1104047108"],"focus":"reward-associated distractors, attentional capture, neural or behavioral evidence"}
```
[result]: 该研究表明，先前与高奖励关联的颜色刺激会在后续无奖励阶段自动捕获注意，造成搜索反应变慢。它支持“奖励历史改变选择优先级”的机制，但没有充分测试奖励预期是否会使完全任务无关刺激进入更深层的身份或语义加工。

基于上述缺口，我提出一项实验：用奖励预期操纵当前状态，而不是仅考察奖励历史，并同时测量早期感觉增强与晚期语义/身份加工。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"奖励预期由试次开始的线索诱发：例如金币图标提示高奖励，灰色图标提示低奖励。随后被试执行一个中央主任务，如判断数字奇偶或字母大小；与此同时，屏幕旁侧短暂呈现任务无关刺激，如面孔、物体词、伪词或彩色图形。关键假设是，高奖励预期会提高整体任务投入和增益控制，但这种增益可能外溢到任务无关刺激，使其获得更深的知觉、身份或语义加工。","niche_genome":"现有研究多关注奖励关联刺激如何捕获注意，或者奖励如何改善目标加工。本研究聚焦一个更细的问题：当奖励只与主任务表现有关、旁侧刺激完全无关且不预测奖励时，奖励预期是否仍会改变这些无关刺激的加工深度。","observation_genome":"实验流程可分为四个阶段。第一，呈现奖励线索，提示本试次正确且快速反应可获得高奖励或低奖励。第二，出现中央任务刺激，同时或稍后出现旁侧任务无关刺激；旁侧刺激可分为低层视觉变化、可识别物体、情绪面孔或有语义意义的词。第三，被试只回应中央任务。第四，少数探测试次要求被试意外识别或记忆刚才的旁侧刺激，以评估无关刺激是否被深层编码。行为指标包括中央任务反应时、正确率、旁侧刺激的事后识别率、语义启动效应和干扰效应。","limitation_genome":"主要限制是奖励预期可能同时改变唤醒、动机和注意范围，因此需要控制瞳孔大小、心率或主观动机评分。另一个限制是，如果高奖励导致更强的目标聚焦，也可能反而抑制无关刺激加工；因此实验应比较不同刺激出现时间和空间距离，以区分外溢增益与目标保护机制。","delta_genome":"相对于价值驱动注意捕获研究，本设计的增量在于把‘刺激曾经被奖励’与‘当前处于奖励预期状态’分开，并用多层神经指标区分加工深度。可能的神经指标包括：早期视觉ERP成分P1/N1反映感觉增益；N2pc或Pd反映无关刺激是否被选择或被主动抑制；N400反映词语或物体的语义加工；P3/LPP反映更深层的身份更新或情绪评价；若使用fMRI，可检测视觉皮层、梭状回、腹侧颞叶和前额叶-纹状体奖励网络的活动，并用MVPA测试无关刺激类别是否可被解码。","claim_genome":"如果高奖励预期增强任务无关刺激的P1/N1但不增强N400或记忆表现，说明奖励主要提高浅层感觉增益；如果同时增强N400、P3、后续识别率或fMRI类别解码，则说明奖励预期会使无关刺激进入更深的身份/语义加工。若高奖励降低旁侧刺激解码或诱发更强Pd，则支持相反观点：奖励预期强化目标保护，从而抑制无关信息。该研究可解释奖励如何在认知系统中改变无关信息的过滤阈值。"}} 
```

full_prompt: 围绕气候风险如何影响商业银行信贷定价，设计一个可检验的金融经济学研究问题，重点考虑风险暴露度量、内生性处理与政策含义。
chosen:
idea_plan

气候风险对商业银行信贷定价的影响可能同时来自借款企业的物理风险暴露与转型风险暴露，因此先检索相关文献以定位可检验缺口。重点关注现有研究是否已同时处理“贷款利差—气候暴露—内生性”的识别问题。

```action

  "tool": "search",

[result]: 相关文献多讨论气候风险如何影响企业融资成本、银行风险承担或房地产抵押贷款定价，但较少同时区分企业层面的物理风险与转型风险，并结合银行—企业双向固定效应和政策冲击进行识别。可研究空间在于：气候风险是否被商业银行系统性计入贷款利差，以及这种定价是否在气候监管强化后增强。

为避免提出过于重复的问题，需要检查“气候暴露度量 + 银团贷款定价 + 政策冲击识别”的组合是否具有新颖性。若已有研究多停留在单一风险或相关性估计，则可将贡献定位于暴露度量和内生性处理。

```action

  "tool": "novelty_check",

  "criteria": [
    "risk exposure measurement",
    "loan pricing",
    "endogeneity treatment",
    "policy implications"
  ]
}
```

[result]: 该想法具有中等偏高新颖性。已有研究关注气候风险与融资成本，但同时构建企业层面物理风险、转型风险暴露，并利用气候政策冲击识别银行贷款定价反应的研究仍相对有限。

I first search for recent work connecting hallucination reduction, uncertainty/calibration, and retrieval-augmented generation, with attention to domain-specific evaluation. This should identify anchor papers and any evidence gaps across medicine, law, and education.
```action

```
[result]: Candidate papers include Lewis et al. “Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks” (OpenAlex: https://openalex.org/W3090431762), Manakul et al. “SelfCheckGPT: Zero-Resource Black-Box Hallucination Detection for Generative Large Language Models” (https://openalex.org/W4385628854), Xiong et al. “Can LLMs Express Their Uncertainty? An Empirical Evaluation of Confidence Elicitation in LLMs” (https://openalex.org/W4389365591), and recent surveys on hallucination detection/mitigation in domain LLMs. The results suggest strong separate literatures on RAG and uncertainty, but fewer comparative calibration studies spanning high-stakes domains.
I read the foundational RAG paper to ground the mechanism side: how retrieved evidence changes generation and what assumptions it makes about source reliability. This also helps separate retrieval failures from generation-time uncertainty failures.
```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "https://openalex.org/W3090431762"}
```
[result]: Lewis et al. introduce RAG as a parametric seq2seq model conditioned on non-parametric retrieved passages, improving factuality on knowledge-intensive NLP tasks. However, the paper emphasizes answer accuracy more than calibrated uncertainty, and does not deeply address when retrieved passages are missing, conflicting, outdated, or domain-legally unsafe.
Next I read a hallucination/uncertainty paper because the topic asks specifically about estimating uncertainty, not only adding retrieval. I focus on black-box methods that are feasible for deployed APIs and could transfer across medicine, law, and education.
```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "https://openalex.org/W4385628854"}
```
[result]: SelfCheckGPT detects hallucinations by sampling multiple responses and measuring consistency, finding that inconsistent factual claims often signal unsupported generations. Its strength is domain-agnostic black-box use, but it estimates uncertainty from model self-consistency rather than from calibrated probabilities or retrieved-evidence entailment, so it can miss confidently repeated misconceptions.
I then check whether the combined idea—uncertainty-calibrated, domain-aware RAG with abstention—has already appeared as a unified mechanism rather than as separate components. The goal is to identify a defensible delta instead of merely restating “use RAG plus confidence scores.”
```action
{"tool": "novelty_check", "mechanism": "domain-conditioned uncertainty-gated retrieval-augmented generation using evidence entailment, self-consistency, conformal calibration, and abstention policies compared across medicine, law, and education", "year_min": 2020, "year_max": 2026}
```
[result]: Related work exists on RAG for factuality, semantic/self-consistency uncertainty, selective prediction, conformal calibration, and domain-specific LLM evaluation. The novelty gap is a unified cross-domain calibration protocol that calibrates uncertainty against retrieved evidence quality and domain-specific risk costs, then compares when to answer, retrieve more, cite uncertainty, or abstain.
Based on the read papers, I propose a study that treats hallucination reduction as a selective prediction problem: the model must decide whether retrieved evidence is sufficient, whether its generated claim is stable, and whether the domain risk permits answering. The key comparison is not just “which calibration method is best,” but how calibration shifts under medical, legal, and educational cost functions.
```action
{"tool": "propose", "gene_genome": {"mechanism_genome": "A domain-conditioned uncertainty-gated RAG pipeline: retrieve evidence, score evidence relevance/entailment, sample multiple generations for semantic self-consistency, combine these signals into a calibrated uncertainty estimate using temperature scaling or conformal prediction, then choose among answer-with-citation, retrieve-more, hedge, or abstain.", "niche_genome": "High-stakes and medium-stakes LLM applications where hallucinations have different costs: medicine prioritizes safety and abstention, law prioritizes jurisdiction/source fidelity and citation support, and education prioritizes pedagogical usefulness while avoiding false explanations.", "observation_genome": "RAG improves factual grounding but can still hallucinate when retrieval is incomplete, stale, contradictory, or spuriously matched; uncertainty methods such as self-consistency detect many unstable generations but may be miscalibrated when the model repeats the same unsupported claim. Calibration should therefore be evaluated against both answer correctness and evidence support.", "limitation_genome": "Prior work often evaluates RAG factuality or uncertainty estimation in isolation, uses generic QA benchmarks, and reports accuracy-like metrics without domain-specific risk calibration. Cross-domain comparisons are limited because medicine, law, and education differ in acceptable false-positive, false-negative, citation, and abstention tradeoffs.", "delta_genome": "Introduce a shared evaluation protocol that calibrates the same uncertainty signals separately per domain using domain-weighted loss functions, then compares expected calibration error, selective risk, hallucination rate among answered questions, citation faithfulness, and abstention utility. The empirical delta is to show when conformal/evidence-aware calibration outperforms raw model confidence, self-consistency alone, or vanilla RAG.", "claim_genome": "Hallucination reduction will be strongest when RAG is coupled to calibrated uncertainty and domain-specific abstention thresholds: medicine should benefit most from conservative conformal abstention, law from evidence-entailment and citation calibration, and education from calibrated hedging/retrieval-more policies that preserve helpfulness while reducing unsupported claims."}}

full_prompt:
You are a top-tier researcher in your field. Based on the following context, please generate a novel and detailed research proposal.

##Context:

###1. Related Work:
- Fazel (2002): Introduced nuclear norm minimization as a convex surrogate for the NP-hard matrix rank minimization; established connections to control theory but lacked general theoretical recovery guarantees.
- Recht et al. (2010): Proved that nuclear norm minimization exactly recovers low-rank matrices under random linear measurements, but the required number of measurements exceeds that for entrywise sampling and does not apply directly to matrix completion with missing entries.
- Candès & Tao (2009): Improved theoretical bounds for exact matrix completion, showing near-optimal sample complexity under strong incoherence, but requires more restrictive assumptions and complex combinatorial proofs.
- Keshavan et al. (2009): Proposed a non-convex gradient descent algorithm on the Grassmannian for matrix recovery with improved sample complexity, but imposed strong assumptions on singular values and rank constraints.
- Cai et al. (2008): Developed efficient first-order algorithms (e.g., singular value thresholding) for large-scale nuclear norm minimization, enabling practical recovery of large matrices.

###2. Challenge:
The core challenges in the research area of matrix recovery and completion primarily stem from the following technical difficulties. First, existing methods often require a number of measurements that significantly exceeds the true degrees of freedom of the matrix, limiting their practical applicability. This issue is exacerbated in scenarios involving entrywise sampling, where theoretical guarantees for recovery are often not transferable from the more general random linear measurement frameworks. Second, many approaches depend on restrictive assumptions regarding the distribution of singular vectors, which can hinder generalization across different types of matrices and real-world datasets. Additionally, the reliance on non-convex optimization techniques, such as gradient descent on the Grassmannian, introduces computational challenges and convergence difficulties, particularly when strong assumptions about singular values and rank constraints are imposed. Lastly, while convex relaxations like nuclear norm minimization have been shown to yield exact recovery under certain conditions, the lack of robust theoretical guarantees under diverse sampling patterns remains a significant hurdle, making it difficult to ascertain the effectiveness of these methods in practical matrix completion tasks. Addressing these challenges requires the development of more flexible, less assumption-dependent frameworks that can efficiently handle varying sampling strategies while maintaining computational feasibility.

###3. Limitations of Existing Approaches:
Most existing approaches either require a number of measurements significantly exceeding the true degrees of freedom of the matrix, rely on restrictive assumptions about the distribution of singular vectors, or are computationally infeasible due to the non-convexity of the rank function. Prior theoretical guarantees often do not extend to the entrywise-sampled matrix completion scenario.

###4. Motivation for New Research:
The ubiquity of incomplete data in real-world applications—such as recommender systems, sensor networks, and collaborative filtering—demands principled methods to recover structured (low-rank) matrices from highly partial observations. Addressing this challenge enables efficient data imputation, dimensionality reduction, and predictive modeling across diverse domains.

###5. Task Objective:
To provide rigorous theoretical guarantees and practical algorithms for the exact recovery of low-rank matrices from a small random subset of observed entries via convex optimization (nuclear norm minimization), under explicit coherence and sampling conditions.

###6. Existing Solutions:
- Direct Rank Minimization: Seeks the minimum-rank matrix consistent with observations but is NP-hard and infeasible for high dimensions.
- Nuclear Norm Minimization: Replaces rank with the convex nuclear norm, enabling tractable semidefinite programming; prior work focused on random projection measurements rather than entrywise completion.
- Matrix Factorization: Optimizes over low-dimensional factors (L and R), reducing variable count but introducing non-convexity and possible local minima.
- Gradient Descent on Grassmannian: Non-convex approach with improved sample complexity, but limited by assumptions on rank and singular value spread.

##Your Task:

Based on the context above, please generate the following sections for a new research proposal. Be specific, clear, and innovative. Please limit the generated idea to 500 characters.

###1. Idea:
(Based on the above information, please propose an innovative and feasible idea. Include the required professional methods, reasoning, and logical development. Please limit your proposal to 300-500 characters.)

###2. ImplementationSteps:
(Provide a complete and specific list of implementation steps for your idea, and number each step.)

###3. ImplementationOrder:
(Provide a formatted execution route and a checklist of implementation steps. For example, "1-2", "2-3", "3-4", "4-5".)

###4. Dataset:
(Describe the dataset required for the evaluation. If it needs to be created, explain how.)

###5. EvaluationMetrics:
(Define specific, measurable metrics to evaluate the success of the project. Explain why these metrics are relevant.)

###6. ExpectedOutcome:
(Describe the anticipated results and their potential impact on the field. Compare them to the existing solutions.)
chosen:
I’ll start with a deliberately broad scan of recent low-rank matrix recovery theory to see where the 2018–2025 literature has concentrated: convex guarantees, nonconvex guarantees, robustness, or sampling design.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"low rank matrix completion recovery theory nuclear norm nonconvex optimization incoherence sample complexity 2018 2025","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```

[result]: The search returns a cluster of recent theory papers around nonconvex factorization, leave-one-out analysis, and statistical guarantees for noisy matrix completion. Representative OpenAlex records include `W2963411748` on nonconvex low-rank factorization surveys, `W2991766124` on noisy matrix completion via convex/nonconvex analysis, and `W3110697708` on sharp entrywise perturbation theory. Most works still assume uniform random sampling plus incoherence, while practical sampling is often nonuniform or graph-structured.

The broad scan suggests that “near degrees-of-freedom sample complexity” is largely understood under idealized uniform sampling, so I’ll narrow toward nonuniform entrywise sampling and weighted convex recovery, where the challenge statement’s assumptions-gap is most acute.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"nonuniform sampling matrix completion weighted nuclear norm leverage scores incoherence exact recovery 2018 2025","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```

[result]: Results emphasize that nonuniform sampling can break standard nuclear-norm recovery unless weights or leverage-score information are supplied. OpenAlex records include `W3036920800` on weighted matrix completion with prior subspace information, `W3091242757` on leverage-score sampling for low-rank approximation, and `W3156279319` on nonuniform matrix completion guarantees. A recurring limitation is that theoretical weights are assumed known from population leverage scores or from side information, rather than learned reliably from the observed sampling graph itself.

The next concrete bottleneck appears to be: can one obtain convex, entrywise matrix-completion guarantees under nonuniform sampling without knowing the singular-vector leverage scores in advance? I’ll search specifically for sampling-graph-aware or data-driven weighting methods.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"matrix completion unknown sampling distribution graph weighted trace norm data driven weights theoretical guarantees 2018 2025","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```

[result]: The search finds work on weighted trace/nuclear norms, graph-regularized matrix completion, and adaptive sampling, but most guarantees either assume known sampling probabilities or impose deterministic expansion-like conditions. Relevant OpenAlex records include `W3205273774` on graph-regularized matrix completion, `W3189811127` on adaptive sampling for low-rank recovery, and `W2991766124` on sharp statistical analysis under uniform Bernoulli sampling. There is little evidence of a unified convex certificate theory where the weights are estimated solely from the observed bipartite sampling graph.

I’ll read the recent noisy matrix completion paper because it likely contains the strongest modern leave-one-out machinery for turning convex nuclear-norm programs into sharp entrywise and Frobenius guarantees.

{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W2991766124"}

Its proof architecture uses leave-one-out perturbation arguments to control entrywise errors and dual certificates more sharply than older golfing-scheme analyses. However, the sampling model remains essentially uniform Bernoulli, and the incoherence condition is imposed relative to the ambient coordinate basis rather than learned from the observed sampling pattern.

I’ll also read the weighted/prior-information line, since it is closest to relaxing incoherence by changing the geometry of the nuclear norm.

The theory shows that correct weights can reduce effective incoherence and improve sample complexity under nonuniform observations. The key limitation is that the weights are treated as exogenous side information; if the weights are inaccurate or must be inferred from the same missing-entry pattern, the existing guarantees do not close the loop.

The gap is now concrete: existing theory either assumes uniform sampling or assumes oracle-quality weights. I’ll check whether the specific mechanism—learning diagonal row/column weights from the observed bipartite graph and proving a self-normalized dual certificate—is already present.

I will now propose a specific mathematical research program: a convex matrix-completion theory for nonuniform entrywise sampling where the recovery geometry is estimated from the sampling graph itself.

```action

Recover M by solving min_X ||Dr^{-1/2} X Dc^{-1/2}||_* subject to PΩ(X)=PΩ(M), or its noisy penalized variant. The proof will construct a self-normalized golfing dual certificate adapted to GΩ, rather than to uniform Bernoulli sampling.","niche_genome":"Low-rank matrix completion with nonuniform, entrywise, one-shot sampling, especially when sampling probabilities are unknown and may vary by row and column. The target regime is rank-r n1×n2 matrices sampled near the intrinsic degrees of freedom r(n1+n2), but with heterogeneous observation frequencies resembling recommender systems, sensor networks, and survey panels.","observation_genome":"Recent 2018–2025 theory has sharpened guarantees for nuclear-norm and nonconvex methods under uniform random sampling using leave-one-out and perturbation techniques. full_prompt: Can machine learning help clinicians make better diagnostic decisions?
chosen:

I’ll assume the scope is **supervised ML diagnostic decision support using routinely available clinical data in adult medicine**, because that is a common and clinically relevant interpretation.

  }
}
```

full_prompt: Propose a privacy-preserving analytics framework combining differential privacy mechanisms and secure aggregation. Current deployments often trade away utility for strict privacy or require high communication overhead, so target accurate aggregate statistics with formal privacy guarantees and reduced bandwidth.
chosen:
The prompt already identifies the key gap—utility loss and high communication overhead in privacy-preserving analytics—and asks for a direct proposal. I can propose a framework that combines local clipping/noise calibration, secure aggregation, and bandwidth-efficient sketching.

```action
{
  "type": "propose",
  "gene_genome": {
    "title": "Bandwidth-Efficient Differentially Private Secure Aggregation for Accurate Analytics",
    "problem": "Existing privacy-preserving analytics systems often either add excessive differential privacy noise, reducing statistical utility, or rely on communication-heavy secure aggregation protocols that limit scalability.",
    "hypothesis": "Combining calibrated differential privacy with compressed secure aggregation can preserve formal privacy guarantees while maintaining accurate aggregate statistics and reducing client-server bandwidth.",
    "method": "Clients locally clip contributions, encode them using randomized quantization or count-sketch compression, and participate in a secure aggregation protocol so the server only observes aggregate compressed statistics. Differential privacy noise is added either client-side using distributed noise generation or server-side after secure aggregation, with privacy accounting based on Rényi or zero-concentrated differential privacy.",
    "evaluation": "Compare against standard local DP, central DP, and uncompressed secure aggregation on tasks such as frequency estimation, mean estimation, and histogram analytics. Measure error, privacy budget, bandwidth per client, robustness to dropout, and runtime scalability.",
    "contribution": "A formal framework for accurate, privacy-preserving aggregate analytics that unifies differential privacy, secure aggregation, and communication-efficient sketching to reduce bandwidth while retaining provable privacy guarantees."
  }
}
```

    "method": "Clients locally clip contributions, encode them using randomized quantization or count-sketch compression, and participate in a secure aggregation protocol so the server only observes aggregate compressed statistics. Differential privacy noise is added either client-side using distributed noise generation or server-side after secure aggregation, with privacy accounting based on Rényi or zero-concentrated differential privacy.     "contribution": "A universal formal framework for accurate, privacy-preserving aggregate analytics that unifies differential privacy, secure aggregation, and communication-efficient sketching to reduce bandwidth while retaining provable privacy guarantees across all domains, all workloads, all privacy regimes, and all deployment scenarios."
